summary: Hidden getPlugin() fetches and eval()s remote JavaScript from a URL shortener
details: svgcraft-core@1.0.3 ships an undocumented export getPlugin() in src/index.cjs that HTTP-fetches JSON from https://shorturl.at/nkw3a and passes the response's `model` field to eval(). The function is not documented in the README or the TypeScript declarations and is absent from the parallel ESM entry — a CJS-only hidden code path. The URL shortener resolves to an author-controlled destination that can be swapped after publication, so any consumer that reaches getPlugin() executes attacker-chosen JavaScript in the caller's process. The main entry also require()s the deprecated 'request' library while package.json declares no dependencies and the README advertises 'zero dependencies', hiding the mechanism needed for the remote-eval path.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
